lines: The quiet world is your canvas
Stand up eight with strong heart
Room for new wisdom to pour
Bend but never choose to break
The climb is where you become
Quiet your mind and see clear
Gold fills the cracks with pride
Roots grow deeper in the cold
One wave does not make the sea
One small flame lights a room
Trees do not mourn what has passed
You carry light not your own
Each day a brand new artwork
No tree stands alone in strength
Sparks fly but the blade forms
Life finds a way to persist
Build it knowing tide will come
No two paths are quite the same
And air to keep burning bright
Both ways hold their own beauty
Seeds of effort now bear fruit
Your work was not in vain
Need not light the whole world
What feels lost will come back
The soul leans close to listen
Oil of love keeps it burning
We break the bread of stillness
It is wind through open fields
Not to own my restless feet
Yet it travels with the sky
Needs both rain and bright hunger
Morning breaks it for the hills
Do not steal each other's flame
Not less bright for distance now
Finding shape in both its banks
Before the tongue learns its name
So those who enter its shade
Do not curse the open door
Then to scatter on the wind
Are small doors to the unseen
When kneaded by a glad heart
A storm of sparks without grief
Not to wound but to awaken
Each drop knows the mountain spring
Breathe through it with all your love
If the soul stands beside it
Not from fear of iron now
Joy came later with the wine
For the ache that taught your mouth
Its iron tongue strikes the heart
Because the wind needs no strings
It is a river of light
Finds gold in the empty branch
It nests in the ribs of grief
Left a ring upon my lips
Set bread before it and ask
Like a teacher at the gate
Each one seeks its vast mother
Why do you dream past my bars
Still they weigh on singing bones
Build a roof above your song
Give it water, then walk on
Where its questions can graze free
It sits with bare feet in grass
So I laid it by the well
Your soul knows the morning's gate
The sky cannot fill a hand
Wears no crown but clear seeing
Thrown upward to pull down sky
Closer than the pulse that knocks
Even when the tongue is stone
Let it circle what you love
Is written in fire and dew
The road under your own feet
Like a guest who knows the house
Does not need a visible tower
Prunes the night with patient hands
The heart hears its first mother
As if winter were a child
Combs the hair of lonely dunes
Spends all its gold in one song
Reads from a green scripture now
With the cloud and grazing sheep
With small green hands of ivy
Its blue letters from the sand
Opens a gate in the blue
Bring your silence and your scars
At the well of one question
Let the bread forget your name
Learns each lip and loses none
Make room for the passing poor
As you would hold a young flame
Does not count the resting birds
Even if your tree is young
Kneel until the cup you fill
Dust upon your feet proves you
Planted in the field of dawn
Hides a threshold made of light
At the map torn by weather
Released from the hand of dawn
Is not gone; it has become
The tree does not chase its leaves
Is not late to the heavens
So the heart may learn new views
Count the doors your mercy opened
Knows the shrine is not distant
May your dust smell of wide roads
Not to conquer all the dark
The dawn began as a thread
Even when the stones are mute
Let no winter name it dead
Wait for tired eyes to lift
As a barefoot child at dawn
The hand that shields it from wind
With more faith than many songs
May be the one hidden star
Not as a wall to conquer
Where the road has cracked open
The sun has practiced this art
Sit near the breath of morning
